items.title: Tune kernel and SVM params jointly
items.avoid: Fixing C=1 and only tweaking γ after the fact.
items.prefer: Grid-search or randomized search over {kernel, C, γ, degree} with stratified cross-validation on the training fold.
items.why: C (margin slack) and γ (RBF locality) interact — joint search finds balanced bias-variance.

items.title: Scale features before distance/dot-product kernels
items.avoid: Feeding raw mixed-unit columns into RBF or polynomial kernels.
items.prefer: StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler fit on training data only, then transform val/test.
items.why: Kernels use ‖x−x′‖² and x·x′; unscaled features skew similarity toward large-magnitude coordinates.

items.title: Match kernel capacity to sample size
items.avoid: High-degree polynomial or tiny γ⁻¹ (very large γ) on datasets with hundreds of points.
items.prefer: Lower degree (2–3), moderate γ, and regularize with smaller C when n is small.
items.why: Flexible kernels need more data to generalize; otherwise validation error spikes.
items.example: n=300 → try degree≤3; n=30k → more freedom may be justified with CV.

items.title: Inspect support vectors, not just accuracy
items.avoid: Reporting only test accuracy after kernel tuning.
items.prefer: Check n_support_, margin violations, and confusion matrix; compare linear vs RBF validation curves.
items.why: Kernel SVMs with many support vectors signal high capacity usage or noisy boundaries.
items.example: clf.n_support_.sum() close to n_train suggests near memorization — revisit γ and C.
